message:
feat(storage): deferred blocked-cache refresh for dependency mutations to reduce DB lock contention

Under concurrent agents, add_dependency and remove_dependency were
causing DB lock contention because each mutation did an eager
blocked-cache rebuild in a second write transaction immediately after
the first commit. The incremental rebuild traverses all parent-child
edges while holding a write lock, blocking other agents.

- Add defer_cache_refresh flag to MutationContext that sets the stale
  marker inside the primary write transaction but skips the eager
  post-commit rebuild, letting ensure_blocked_cache_fresh handle it
  lazily on the next read
- Add BlockedCacheRefreshPlan::Deferred variant that is applied as a
  no-op after commit (stale marker already written in-transaction)
- Add invalidate_cache_deferred() convenience method on MutationContext
- Wire dep add/remove to use deferred invalidation instead of eager
  incremental refresh
- Update post-commit dispatch to handle Deferred plan with debug logging
- Update Cargo.lock (crc32c added to fsqlite-mvcc)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
